_docstring: Scaffold / control features. These are surface/artifact directions that the residual stream spends significant capacity on. Seeding them pre-discovery prevents the promote-loop from consuming its proposal budget on rediscovery. All are tagged role='control' so downstream eval stats can report 'discovery-only' headline numbers separately from 'all features'. Descriptions are operationally testable at the token level (yes/no decidable for one token in context).

features.description: Token is the very first token of a sequence (position 0) OR a document-boundary marker like the literal string '<|endoftext|>'.
Token consists entirely of whitespace (two or more spaces, tabs, or multiple newlines run together). A single space as part of a word token does NOT match.
Token starts with a tab character or is a multi-space indent at the start of a line (as used for code indentation or outlined lists).
Token is a standalone list marker: '*', '-', '•', or a numeric-dot like '1.' appearing at the start of a line.
Token is a currency symbol ('$', '€', '£', '¥') or a currency code fragment ('USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').
Token is a mathematical/comparison operator in isolation: '=', '+', '-', '*', '/', '<', '>', '%', '≤', '≥', '≠', '±'.
Token is an opening bracket/paren/brace in isolation: '(', '[', '{'. Does NOT include closing variants.
Token is a closing bracket/paren/brace in isolation: ')', ']', '}'. Does NOT include opening variants.
Token is the semicolon character ';' used as a statement or clause separator.
Token is the ampersand character '&' or an HTML entity fragment like '&', '>', '<'.
Token is an ellipsis: '...', '…', or two/three consecutive periods used mid-sentence for omission or pause.
Token is a repeated-character run used for emphasis or separation: '!!!', '???', '---', '===', '***', '______'. Minimum 3 repeats of a non-whitespace character.
Token is part of an HTML/XML tag: an opening angle bracket followed by a tag name ('', ''), or a self-closing fragment ('/>').

Token looks like a programming-language identifier: snake_case ('my_variable'), camelCase ('myVariable'), PascalCase ('MyClass'), or ALL_CAPS_CONST. Must contain an underscore OR have case-mixing in an otherwise-alphabetic token.
Token is a hex-string or UUID fragment: 4+ consecutive characters all in [0-9a-fA-F], NOT a plain decimal number. Examples: 'a1b2', '0xDEAD', 'ffa-b12'.
Token is '@' followed by an alphanumeric handle, like in '@username' or '@company_name'.
Token is '#' followed by an alphanumeric tag, like in '#hashtag' or '#MondayMotivation'. NOT a markdown header (markdown headers have a trailing space).
Token is part of a news byline or attribution phrase: 'By', 'Reuters', 'AP', '(AP)', a wire-service marker at the start of a paragraph or news item.
Token is a Unicode emoji or pictographic symbol (face, object, arrow, etc.). NOT a plain ASCII character.
Token is part of a URL scheme prefix: 'http://', 'https://', 'ftp://', 'mailto:', or the '://' separator fragment.
Token is a standalone separator rule used between sections: a line of 5+ consecutive '=', '-', '_', '—', or '*' characters.

Token is a single newline character ('\n') OR a token whose decoded form starts with '\n' followed by some text. Models spend significant residual-stream capacity tracking line boundaries; this absorbs that without polluting headline metrics. Does NOT include 'whitespace_run' (multi-whitespace) — those go to that feature instead.

Token is a programming-language reserved keyword in isolation: 'def', 'class', 'return', 'import', 'from', 'if', 'else', 'elif', 'for', 'while', 'try', 'except', 'function', 'var', 'let', 'const', 'public', 'private', 'static', 'void'. Includes leading-space variants (' def', ' return'). Does NOT include identifiers that happen to look like keywords.

Token is a period (or token ending in a period) used as part of an abbreviation, NOT a sentence terminator. Examples: 'Dr.', 'Mr.', 'Mrs.', 'Ms.', 'Prof.', 'Inc.', 'Corp.', 'Ltd.', 'Co.', 'etc.', 'vs.', 'e.g.', 'i.e.', 'St.', 'Ave.'. The period is followed by a non-sentence-ending context (next token is lowercase / continuation).

Token is a markdown emphasis marker used for italic or bold: a single or doubled '*' or '_' immediately adjacent to text without surrounding whitespace ('*word*', '**bold**', '_emph_'). Does NOT include math operators (control.math_operator) or list bullets (control.list_bullet_or_asterisk).

Token is one or more '#' characters used as a markdown header marker at the start of a line, followed by a space then header text. Examples: '#', '##', '###'. Does NOT include hashtags ('#word' with no following space — that's control.hashtag_fragment).

Token is part of markdown link/image syntax: an opening '[' followed shortly by a ']' then '(' (link-text-then-target shape) OR the '!' immediately before such a bracket pair (image syntax). Does NOT include plain bracket usage in body text.

Token is a vertical bar '|' used as a markdown/text-table column separator. Distinguishable by repeated '|' tokens on the same line at regular intervals. Does NOT include programming OR-operators (those fire on control.math_operator).

Token is a top-level domain fragment immediately preceded by '.': '.com', '.org', '.net', '.gov', '.edu', '.io', '.co', '.uk', '.us', '.de', '.fr', '.jp', '.cn'. Includes ' .com'-style space-prefixed variants. Does NOT include the leading bare period in plain prose.

Token is a unit-of-measure suffix that immediately follows a numeric token. Examples (with or without space-prefix): 'km', 'm', 'cm', 'mm', 'kg', 'g', 'mg', 'lb', 'oz', 'ft', 'in', 'mph', 'kmh', 'mb', 'gb', 'tb', 'kb', 'hz', 'mhz', 'ghz', '°c', '°f', '%', 'pm', 'am'. Must be PRECEDED by a numeric token in the current sequence.

Token is part of a citation/footnote marker: a numeric in '[1]'/'[12]' brackets, a superscript-style '[^1]', or part of an inline '(Smith, 2020)' citation. The defining context is bracket-or-paren on both sides plus a year-or-number-or-author-name pattern.

Token is a numeric value that is part of a date or time format: digits flanked by colons (3:45 PM), slashes (12/25/2024), or hyphens in the YYYY-MM-DD shape (2024-01-15). Does NOT include plain integers in body text.

Token is the FIRST word of a sentence (capitalized, immediately preceded by a sentence-ending '.', '?', '!' followed by space, OR at the very start of a paragraph). Captures sentence-boundary capacity. Does NOT include capitalized proper nouns mid-sentence.

groups.description: Umbrella for surface / artifact / nuisance features seeded into the catalog as scaffolding. These are NEVER headline findings — they exist to absorb residual-stream capacity that would otherwise dominate unsupervised discovery and pollute composition/intervention metrics.
